mission: Define the Phase 0 verification ladder, ring-promotion law, and candidate-bound environment policy so compile, simulation, runtime publication, preprod rehearsal, canary, widening, pause, rollback, and recovery proof all consume the same exact release tuple.
source_precedence: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0A Foundation kernel, control plane, and hard invariants

gateDefinitions.continuityRule: all affected continuity families require exact coverage, including newer families from findings 104-111
gateDefinitions.essentialFunctionRule: board and alert wiring for the affected essential functions must compile in the same tuple
gateDefinitions.blockingCondition: route, continuity, or embedded coverage may not be stale or inferred from adjacent contracts
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 104 - The admin control plane still treated continuity proof as optional release commentary

gateDefinitions.continuityRule: same-shell recovery and route-intent law must be proven end to end
gateDefinitions.essentialFunctionRule: patient and staff journeys in scope must each pass at least one reference case
gateDefinitions.blockingCondition: generic browser success is insufficient if recovery disposition or settlement proof drifts

gateDefinitions.continuityRule: disclosure-safe telemetry and route continuity remain in force during security tests
gateDefinitions.essentialFunctionRule: critical-path SLI and alert thresholds must have preprod evidence
gateDefinitions.blockingCondition: unreviewed security findings, missing provenance, or unsafe telemetry schemas block promotion

gateDefinitions.continuityRule: constrained and frozen journeys must be proven on the same tuple as ordinary-live posture
gateDefinitions.essentialFunctionRule: every essential function needs restore, failover, chaos, and runbook freshness references
gateDefinitions.blockingCondition: stale rehearsal evidence or tuple drift downgrades readiness and blocks canary
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 112 - Resilience restore authority still depended on loose runbooks and dashboards

gateDefinitions.continuityRule: watch, governance, operations, and recovery surfaces all read the same continuity and release proof
gateDefinitions.essentialFunctionRule: widening is legal only when affected essential functions have exact live proof and fresh recovery authority
gateDefinitions.blockingCondition: parity drift, watch tuple drift, stale readiness, unresolved continuity proof, or missing provenance blocks widen or resume
source_refs: forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 95 - The audit still omitted governance watch-tuple parity and recovery posture from release oversight
blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2E. Telephony call-session ingestion, evidence readiness, and convergence
blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2B. NHS login bridge and local session engine
blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6D. Referral pack composer, dispatch adapters, and transport contract

environmentBaselineFingerprints.notes: The baseline fingerprint is mandatory evidence for every ring advancement; tuple drift forces restart or halt semantics.

ringPolicies.purpose: Compile the candidate tuple, simulate config, and freeze the initial cross-layer matrix before any preview publication.
entryPrerequisites: Gate 0 static, unit, config compilation, config simulation, and standards watchlist proof pinned to one candidate tuple.
One exact ReleaseContractVerificationMatrix assembled before any route-local checks are considered valid.
One aligned EnvironmentBaselineFingerprint created from runtime topology, release freeze, and compilation tuple evidence.
promotionBlockers: Any matrix member, watchlist hash, config simulation tuple, parity tuple, or recovery tuple drift forces restart or halt.
ringPolicies.notes: Local remains candidate-bound and never authorizes live-wave inference. Gate 0 stays an entry prerequisite rather than a separate promotion lane.

ringPolicies.purpose: Prove published preview parity against the same exact tuple and keep browser posture constrained until integration evidence is current.
entryPrerequisites: Local candidate tuple remains immutable; no rebuild-from-source is allowed between local and preview.
PromotionIntentEnvelope stays live and references the same EnvironmentBaselineFingerprint and ReleaseApprovalFreeze.
Preview publication binds the same frontend manifests, design bundles, and route coverage digests as the local matrix.
promotionBlockers: Any matrix member, watchlist hash, config simulation tuple, parity tuple, or recovery tuple drift forces restart or halt.
ringPolicies.notes: Preview proves exact publication parity but still treats calm live trust as blocked until downstream accessibility and recovery proof stay current.

ringPolicies.purpose: Exercise simulator-backed routes, callbacks, and end-to-end recovery on the pinned tuple rather than on adjacent manifests.
entryPrerequisites: Preview parity remains exact or restart is required before integration evidence can settle.
Simulator-backed adapter families and callback replay rules stay attached to the same VerificationScenario.
Continuity-sensitive workflows in scope publish exact ContinuityContractCoverageRecord rows before Gate 2 may pass.
ringPolicies.notes: Integration is the first ring where simulator-backed flows, callback replay, and continuity controls become end-to-end promotion evidence.

ringPolicies.purpose: Rehearse performance, security, resilience, restore, and failover against the same frozen release and watch tuple.
entryPrerequisites: Integration proof remains current and restart-on-drift semantics stay armed.
OperationalReadinessSnapshot, restore runs, failover runs, and runbook bindings are captured against the same release matrix.
SyntheticRecoveryCoverageRecord rows exist for every continuity-sensitive workflow touched by the rehearsal scope.
ringPolicies.notes: Preprod must carry operational readiness, recovery rehearsal freshness, and restore compatibility on the same tuple as route and design proof.

ringPolicies.purpose: Control canary, widen, halt, rollback, and resume only through the declared watch tuple, wave fence, and live proof set.
entryPrerequisites: Preprod freeze, runtime publication, and provenance state still match the pinned ReleaseContractVerificationMatrix hash.
WaveEligibilitySnapshot and WaveControlFence are current for the exact watch tuple being widened or rolled back.
Live wave decisions remain machine-readable and may only settle through the current watch tuple and guardrail posture.
ringPolicies.notes: Production wave control is fail-closed. Drift may only widen into halt, rollback, or restart semantics, never into silent continuation.

assumptions.summary: ConfigCompilationRecord and ConfigSimulationEnvelope refs are published as seq_058 contract-layer objects because earlier tasks froze hashes and bundle refs but not standalone runtime records.
Simulator-backed adapter families remain valid promotion evidence in all Phase 0 rings, including production-wave controls, until later live-cutover tasks publish narrower provider-specific proof.

defects.summary: Integration promotion is fail-closed because the pinned verification tuple drifted.
Preprod promotion is fail-closed because the pinned verification tuple drifted.
Production promotion is fail-closed because the pinned verification tuple drifted.
